Serving All of Guadalupe County, TX

Guadalupe County Stucco Services

Guadalupe County is growing fast, and with that growth comes demand for quality exterior finishes that stand the test of time. Whether you are building a new home in Cibolo, maintaining an established property in Seguin, or upgrading the exterior of a commercial building along the I-10 corridor, our stucco services are built to meet the needs of this expanding region.

Get a Free Stucco Estimate Call Now: (512) 706-9699

Serving a Growing Guadalupe County

The stretch of Guadalupe County between Seguin and the San Antonio metro area has seen remarkable development over the past decade. New neighborhoods are going up in Schertz, Cibolo, and Marion at a rapid pace, and many of these homes feature stucco exteriors that need to be installed right from day one. At the same time, older homes in Seguin and the county's rural communities require upkeep and repairs to address years of weather exposure.

Our team works across the full spectrum of stucco needs in Guadalupe County. On new construction, we collaborate with builders to ensure the stucco system is installed with correct moisture management, control joints, and finishing details. On existing homes, we diagnose and repair cracks, water damage, and aesthetic issues that develop naturally over time.

Stucco Services We Offer in Guadalupe County

Professional stucco and plaster solutions for homes and businesses throughout Guadalupe County.

Stucco Repair

Homes throughout Seguin and the surrounding communities develop stucco issues ranging from minor surface cracks to sections where the finish has detached from the wall. We repair each problem area with materials that bond securely and match the surrounding texture.

Learn More

Stucco Installation

New subdivisions in Cibolo, Schertz, and Marion are choosing stucco for its durability and low maintenance. We install complete stucco systems on new construction, coordinating with builders to get the timing right and the details correct from the start.

Learn More

Stucco Crack Repair

Guadalupe County soils vary from sandy loam near the Guadalupe River to heavier clay further from the floodplain. Both soil types contribute to foundation movement that shows up as cracks in stucco walls. We assess the soil conditions and use appropriate repair strategies.

Learn More

Stucco Water Damage Repair

Poorly sealed stucco on both new and older Guadalupe County homes can allow moisture behind the wall during heavy rains. We trace the water entry point, repair or replace compromised substrate material, and restore the stucco finish with improved moisture protection.

Learn More

Exterior Plaster

Custom homes and estates around Seguin and the Lake McQueeney area often call for refined exterior plaster finishes. We apply a range of smooth and textured plaster systems that deliver the upscale look homeowners want while standing up to South Texas conditions.

Learn More

EIFS Repair & Installation

Commercial and mixed-use developments along the I-10 and I-35 corridors through Guadalupe County frequently use EIFS. We install new systems and repair existing ones, addressing common issues like moisture infiltration at joints and impact damage at ground level.

Learn More

Commercial Stucco

The commercial growth in Schertz, Cibolo, and along FM 78 has created steady demand for commercial stucco work. We handle projects on retail buildings, medical offices, and mixed-use developments, delivering consistent quality on schedule.

Learn More

Why Guadalupe County Builders and Homeowners Work With Us

Proven track record on new construction projects with major Guadalupe County builders

Repair expertise on homes ranging from brand new to several decades old

Knowledge of local soil and climate conditions that affect stucco performance

Coordination with general contractors, project managers, and homeowners

Attention to detail on finish work that ensures a polished final appearance

Warranty-backed installations and repairs for long-term peace of mind

Local Expertise

We understand Guadalupe County soil types, weather patterns, and building codes for every community.

Fully Licensed & Insured

Licensed Texas contractor with full liability and workers' compensation coverage for every Guadalupe County project.

Fast Response

Prompt estimates and scheduling for all Guadalupe County communities. Most projects begin within 1–2 weeks.

Common Stucco Problems We Fix in Guadalupe County

Guadalupe County's mix of new construction and established homes presents a range of stucco challenges. Here are the issues we diagnose and repair most frequently when working on properties from Seguin to Schertz.

Cracks

Even relatively new homes in Guadalupe County develop stucco cracks as fresh foundations settle into the local soil. Older homes near the Guadalupe River contend with additional stress from seasonal changes in the water table.

Bubbling or soft stucco

On new construction where the stucco was applied before the building envelope was fully sealed, or on older homes with degraded weather barriers, trapped moisture causes the finish to bubble and lose adhesion.

Water stains

Irrigation overspray from newly landscaped yards in Cibolo and Schertz subdivisions is a frequent cause of water staining on stucco. Hard water minerals leave white, chalky deposits that worsen with each watering cycle.

Window and door leaks

Rapid construction timelines in fast-growing areas sometimes result in window and door installations that are not perfectly sealed against the stucco. These gaps become apparent during the first heavy rainstorm.

Failing patches

Builder warranty repairs done hastily during the punch-list phase often fail within a few years. The patch material may not match, or the underlying cause of the damage was not properly addressed before the patch was applied.

Separation around trim

Composite and PVC trim on newer homes and traditional wood trim on older Seguin properties both develop gaps against stucco as materials respond differently to South Texas heat.

Impact damage

Construction activity in neighboring lots, lawn care equipment, and everyday bumps from bikes and toys in driveways create chips and gouges that break the stucco surface and expose the substrate to weather.

Faded or stained exterior walls

Guadalupe County receives intense sun exposure for much of the year. Colored stucco finishes on south- and west-facing walls lose vibrancy within a few years without UV-resistant coatings or periodic maintenance.

How We Work in Guadalupe County

A clear, straightforward process from first call to finished project.

1

Schedule Inspection

Get in touch to arrange a visit at your Guadalupe County property. We serve Seguin, Schertz, Cibolo, Marion, and every community across the county.

2

Review Scope

We inspect every wall surface, check moisture levels at key points, and identify both visible damage and potential hidden issues. For new construction, we review the builder's stucco specifications and installation quality.

3

Get Estimate

You receive a straightforward estimate covering labor, materials, and project duration. For builder warranty or insurance-related work, we provide detailed documentation to support your claim.

4

Complete Work

We execute the project on schedule, maintain a clean work area throughout, and conduct a final quality check before handing the finished work over to you.
